source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” guests can create tickets; record their chats in a separate location; never touch Ash's logs/queue.
title: Profiles: guest ticket area + per-profile chat/log isolation
component: tools/tools_tickets.py (or wherever create_ticket lives), agent/turn_log.py, scripts/sprint.py, tools/tools_obsidian.py
current_state: create_ticket writes to the shared tickets/open/. Turn logs all go to one flat logs/turns.jsonl keyed by session_id only; raw_wiki (L1) is global. sprint.py processes tickets/open/ (it already hard-refuses god paths). Nothing separates a guest's tickets, chats, or logs from Ash's.
target_state: Guest-created tickets land in a separate area (tickets/profiles/<name>/) and are NEVER auto-executed: sprint.py only processes Ash's tickets/open/ (extend the existing god-refusal guard to also skip tickets/profiles/*). Per-profile turn logs go to logs/profiles/<name>/turns.jsonl; guest turns are excluded from Ash's global logs/turns.jsonl and from raw_wiki (guest namespace already => _NoopSupabase, so L1 raw stays in the guest's own SQLite). The guest's conversation transcripts live under the guest's vault (vault/<name>/) so 'their chats are recorded in a different folder' as requested.
migration_plan: 1. create_ticket: when current_profile.is_guest, write to tickets/profiles/<name>/ instead of tickets/open/. Tag the ticket with 'profile': <name>.

2. sprint.py: restrict its open-ticket scan to tickets/open/ only and explicitly skip tickets/profiles/* (mirror the existing tickets/god/ refusal). A guest ticket reaches Ash's real queue only if Ash moves it manually.
3. agent/turn_log.append_turn: route to logs/profiles/<name>/turns.jsonl when current_profile is a guest; keep Ash on logs/turns.jsonl. Add the profile name to the entry for traceability.
4. Confirm guest L1/L2/L3 writes target the guest DB only (covered by T-221) so raw_wiki/Supabase never sees guest data.

5. Tests: testing/test_profile_isolation.py â€” guest create_ticket lands in tickets/profiles/<name>/ not tickets/open/; sprint.py planning ignores guest tickets; guest turns write to logs/profiles/<name>/ and NOT logs/turns.jsonl; guest memory writes never appear in pi.db or raw_wiki.
risk_notes: The critical guarantee is that guest activity cannot enter any autonomous-execution path. Re-use sprint.py's existing path-refusal style rather than inventing a new filter. Verify the turn-log split doesn't break the existing offline-safe append/rotation behavior.
